task_completion.description: Load the 'Exam Anxiety' dataset into JASP.
task_completion.visual_evidence: JASP is open and the 'Exam Anxiety' dataset is visible in the data view, showing columns like 'Exam', 'Anxiety', and 'Revise'.

task_completion.description: Configure the Random Forest Regression with the correct target, predictors, and parameters.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The JASP Machine Learning module is open to Random Forest Regression. 'Exam' is set as the Target. 'Anxiety' and 'Revise' are set as Predictors. In the settings/training parameters, the training data split is set to 80% and the number of trees is set to 100.

task_completion.description: Include the predictive performance plot and variable importance in the analysis.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The JASP results panel displays the Random Forest Regression output, specifically showing a 'Predictive Performance' plot and a 'Variable Importance' table or plot.

task_completion.description: Save the JASP project to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The JASP window title bar shows 'rf_exam_analysis.jasp' or a file manager/save dialog confirms the file is saved at '/home/ga/Documents/JASP/rf_exam_analysis.jasp'.

task_completion.description: Create a text report with the Test Set MSE, Test Set R2, and Most Important Predictor in the specified format.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor is open showing a file named 'rf_report.txt' (saved in /home/ga/Documents/JASP/) containing exactly three lines: 'Line 1: MSE: <value>', 'Line 2: R2: <value>', and 'Line 3: Most Important Predictor: <variable name>'. The variable name must be either 'Anxiety' or 'Revise'.

integrity.description: The agent used the JASP GUI to perform the analysis.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the actual JASP application interface being interacted with, rather than a terminal script or Python code bypassing the UI.

integrity.description: The values in the text report match the actual JASP analysis results.
integrity.visual_evidence: The MSE and R2 values typed into 'rf_report.txt' correspond exactly to the Test Set Evaluation metrics visible in the JASP results panel.

integrity.description: The agent did not create the report without running the model.
integrity.visual_evidence: There is visual evidence of the JASP model running and producing results before or while the text report is being written.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected JASP path: /home/ga/Documents/JASP/rf_exam_analysis.jasp. Expected report path: /home/ga/Documents/JASP/rf_report.txt. Valid predictors are 'Anxiety' and 'Revise'. Note: Due to the small dataset size (n=103) and random seed variance, the Test Set MSE typically ranges from 300 to 1100, and the Test Set R2 is frequently negative. Do not penalize the agent for 'poor' model performance (e.g., negative R2 or high MSE) as long as the reported values accurately reflect the JASP output.
